_comment: Illustrative few-shot anchored on docs/icu-notes-two-approaches.md. Input is the full pre-LLM context (EMR Backstory + Dictation transcript). Output is the structured Systems Approach note. Used to anchor format, not to memorise content.
input:
EMR Backstory
=============
Demographics: Mrs Wilson, 68F, ICU day 3
Admission reason: Community-acquired pneumonia, septic shock

Previous notes:
Admitted overnight via ED. CAP with septic shock. Intubated for hypoxic respiratory failure on arrival to ICU. Noradrenaline started at 0.05 mcg/kg/min on D1, peaked at 0.25 D1 evening, weaning since. Cefepime + azithromycin commenced; pending tracheal and blood cultures. CXR with bilateral basal consolidation. ICU day 3 today.

Dictation transcript
====================
[clip 1, 00:00]
Neuro — sedated on propofol, RASS minus 2, GCS not assessable. No focal neurology. Fentanyl infusion for analgesia. Plan to lighten in the morning with a view to SBT.

CVS — noradrenaline weaning, now 0.08 mcg/kg/min, was 0.25 on day 1. MAP in the 70s, lactate 1.8, peak was 4.2. Continue weaning, target off pressors by tomorrow if the trajectory holds.

[clip 2, 00:00]
Resp — AC/VC, FiO₂ 0.4, PEEP 8, P/F ratio 240. Right basal consolidation improving on chest X-ray. SBT tomorrow if off pressors.

Renal — oliguric AKI, creatinine 280, urea 24, potassium 5.4. No RRT yet. Trigger for CRRT would be rising K, worsening acidosis, or fluid overload limiting respiratory wean.

[clip 3, 00:00]
GI — NG feeds tolerated at 30 mils per hour. Bowels not opened day 3, aperient charted.

Heme-ID — haemoglobin 92, stable. Blood cultures grew strep pneumoniae, rationalise pip-taz to benzylpenicillin per micro. Day 3 antibiotics.

Endo — variable-rate insulin, target BSL 6 to 10.

Skin — early sacral pressure redness. Turning regimen, dynamic mattress in place.

Lines — CVC, arterial line, IDC all day 3. Reviewed, all stay. Reassess day 5.

Plan — improving septic shock. Wean noradrenaline overnight, lighten sedation in the morning, target extubation in 24 to 48 hours. Renal function is the watch item; if creatinine or potassium climb, call before they cross threshold.

output.neuro: Sedated on propofol with RASS −2; GCS not assessable. No focal neurology. Fentanyl infusion for analgesia. Plan to lighten sedation in the morning with a view to SBT [clip 1].
output.cvs: Noradrenaline weaning, 0.08 mcg/kg/min (peak 0.25 on D1). MAP in 70s, lactate 1.8 (peak 4.2). Continue weaning; target off pressors by tomorrow if trajectory holds [clip 1].
output.resp: AC/VC, FiO₂ 0.4, PEEP 8, P/F 240. Right basal consolidation improving on CXR. SBT tomorrow if off pressors [clip 2].
output.renal: Oliguric AKI: creatinine 280, urea 24, K⁺ 5.4. No RRT yet. CRRT triggers would be rising K⁺, worsening acidosis, or fluid overload limiting respiratory wean [clip 2].
output.gi: NG feeds tolerated at 30 mL/hr. Bowels not opened on D3; aperient charted [clip 3].
output.heme_id: Hb 92, stable. Blood cultures: Strep. pneumoniae — rationalise pip-taz to benzylpenicillin per micro. Day 3 of antibiotics [clip 3].
output.skin: Early sacral pressure redness. Turning regimen and dynamic mattress in place [clip 3].
output.lines: CVC, arterial line and IDC all day 3 in situ. Reviewed, all stay; reassess day 5 [clip 3].
output.plan: Improving septic shock. Wean noradrenaline overnight, lighten sedation AM, target extubation in 24–48 h. Watch-item is renal function — if Cr or K⁺ climb, call before they cross threshold [clip 3].
